description_long: This object is a shovel with a long, straight wooden handle and a robust, angular metal blade. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of light-colored wood, extending seamlessly into the blade without any visible grip or ergonomic features. The blade is distinctly geometric, with a flat, rectangular face and sharply beveled edges, giving it a modern, faceted appearance. The blade is metallic, likely steel, and is attached firmly to the handle, with no visible fasteners or reinforcements. The overall design is utilitarian, with clean lines and a focus on durability. There are no visible markings, logos, or signs of wear, suggesting the shovel is new or well-maintained.
description: A shovel with a straight wooden handle and a faceted, rectangular metal blade, featuring a clean, modern design with no visible markings or wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the rectangular metal blade and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the seamless transition from handle to blade.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back face of the blade and the handle alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's thin profile and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with no visible grip or ergonomic features.
attribute_blade_type: Rectangular, flat metal blade with sharply beveled, faceted edges and a geometric profile.
